title: TC-FT-001: Fit Test — Normal execution completes successfully (baseline)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds (Emu/Tern/Osprey) connected to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
Wait for audio playback to complete (~10-15 sec).
Observe result screen.
expected: Fit Test completes without interruption. Result screen displays seal quality (e.g., Good/Poor) with per-ear indication. No error dialogs. 'Done' or 'Retry' button is visible and tappable.

title: TC-FT-002: Fit Test — Primary earbud button press DURING audio playback (Emu/Tern/Osprey)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds connected, app open on Fit Test screen.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
While audio playback is active (within first 5 seconds), press the button on the PRIMARY earbud (left/right as defined by device).
Observe Fit Test UI immediately after button press.
Observe Fit Test result screen after audio stops.
Navigate away (Device → Audio Settings) and return to Fit Test.
expected: Fit Test does NOT freeze or crash. Result is either: (a) displayed correctly if button press did not corrupt measurement, OR (b) shows 'Incomplete' / 'Interrupted' state. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): Fit Test result shows incorrect seal value (e.g., Good when seal was broken by button press motion) OR app hangs on 'Running...' indefinitely. App must NOT remain stuck in 'Running' state. After navigating away and back, Fit Test must show idle/ready state (not 'Running').

title: TC-FT-003: Fit Test — Secondary earbud button press DURING audio playback (Emu/Tern/Osprey)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds connected, app open on Fit Test screen.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
While audio playback is active, press the button on the SECONDARY earbud (the non-primary side).
Observe Fit Test UI immediately.
Observe Fit Test result screen.
Attempt to restart Fit Test immediately after.
expected: 🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY: Behavior may differ from primary earbud press. Expected: Fit Test either completes with valid result OR shows 'Interrupted'. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): Secondary button press causes asymmetric failure — e.g., primary press works but secondary press corrupts state, or vice versa. Restarting Fit Test must be possible without app restart.

title: TC-FT-004: Fit Test — Double/triple button press DURING audio playback (Emu/Tern/Osprey)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds connected, app open on Fit Test screen.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
While audio playback is active, rapidly press the earbud button 2-3 times.
Observe UI behavior during rapid presses.
Observe final Fit Test result.
Check app state: navigate to Home, then back to Device → Fit Test.
expected: 🧠 IMMEDIATE STATE TRIGGER: Rapid button presses during Fit Test must not cause app crash or ANR. Expected: Fit Test either completes, shows 'Interrupted', or ignores the button events. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): Rapid presses cause inconsistent result capture (sometimes recorded, sometimes not) or leave Fit Test in a zombie state where 'Start' button is disabled but test is not running. After navigation round-trip, Fit Test must return to ready state.

title: TC-FT-005: Fit Test — Button press at EXACT moment of audio completion (Emu/Tern/Osprey)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds connected, app open on Fit Test screen.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
Wait until the final ~1 second of Fit Test audio playback.
Press the earbud button at the exact moment audio ends.
Observe result screen.
Check if result is saved by navigating away and back.
expected: 🧠 STATE TRIGGER (edge timing): Fit Test must handle concurrent button press at state transition boundary. Expected: Result screen appears normally OR shows 'Interrupted'. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): Button press at audio completion causes result to be lost (no result shown, stuck on blank screen) OR result is shown but navigating away and back loses it (persistence failure).

title: TC-FT-006: Fit Test — Button press → immediate app backgrounding (Emu/Tern/Osprey)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds connected, app open on Fit Test screen.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
While audio playback is active, press the earbud button.
IMMEDIATELY press Home button to background the app (within 1 second of button press).
Wait 5 seconds, then reopen the app from recent apps.
Observe Fit Test state.
Navigate to Device → Fit Test.
expected: 🧠 DEEP LIFECYCLE: App must recover gracefully from concurrent button press + backgrounding during Fit Test. Expected: Fit Test shows either completed result, 'Interrupted' state, or idle/ready state. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): App shows Fit Test stuck in 'Running' state after reopening, or UI is frozen, or app crashes on resume. Must be able to start a new Fit Test without force-killing the app.

title: TC-FT-007: Fit Test — Button press → immediate app force-kill (Emu/Tern/Osprey)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds connected, app open on Fit Test screen.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
While audio playback is active, press the earbud button.
IMMEDIATELY force-kill the app (swipe from recent apps or Settings → Force Stop).
Reopen the app and reconnect to earbuds.
Navigate to: Device → Fit Test.
expected: 🧠 IMMEDIATE STATE TRIGGER + DEEP LIFECYCLE: App must not crash on relaunch. Expected: Fit Test shows idle/ready state (not 'Running'). 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): App crashes on launch, or Fit Test remains in 'Running' state permanently, or the app cannot reconnect to earbuds after force-kill during active Fit Test + button press.

title: TC-FT-008: Fit Test — Button press during Fit Test on Emu vs. Tern vs. Osprey (cross-device comparison)
steps: Precondition: Run this test on EACH device: Emu, Tern, Osprey (separate sessions).
For each device: Connect earbuds, navigate to Device → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
While audio playback is active, press the primary earbud button.
Record result for each device: (a) Did Fit Test complete? (b) Was result correct? (c) Any crash/freeze?
Compare results across all three devices.
expected: 🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY: All three devices (Emu, Tern, Osprey) must exhibit consistent behavior during button-press interruption of Fit Test. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): One device shows different behavior (e.g., Emu crashes, Tern shows wrong result, Osprey works correctly). All must either complete successfully or show 'Interrupted' — no crashes, no incorrect seal values, no stuck states.

title: TC-FT-009: Fit Test — Consecutive runs with button press on first run (Emu/Tern/Osprey)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds connected, app open on Fit Test screen.
Run 1: Tap 'Start Fit Test', press earbud button during audio, observe result (interrupted or completed).
Run 2: Immediately tap 'Start Fit Test' again WITHOUT navigating away.
Do NOT press any buttons during Run 2.
Observe Run 2 result.
Run 3: Tap 'Start Fit Test' again, press earbud button during audio.
Observe Run 3 result.
expected: 🧠 PERSISTENCE CHECK: After a button-press interruption, the Fit Test must be restartable cleanly. Expected: Run 2 (no interruption) completes normally with valid result. Run 3 (with interruption) behaves same as Run 1. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): After interrupted Run 1, Run 2 fails to start (button grayed out) or shows incorrect result, or Run 3 causes crash. Each run must be independent and not corrupt subsequent runs.

title: TC-FT-010: Fit Test — Button press during Fit Test while media is playing in background (Emu/Tern/Osprey)
steps: Precondition: Earbuds connected, media app (e.g., Spotify/YouTube) playing audio in background.
Navigate to: Device → Fit Test.
Tap 'Start Fit Test'.
While Fit Test audio is playing (overlapping with background media), press the earbud button.
Observe Fit Test result.
Check media playback state after Fit Test completes/interrupts.
Navigate away and back to Fit Test.
expected: 🧠 CONCURRENT OPERATIONS: Fit Test must handle button press while both Fit Test audio AND background media are active. Expected: Fit Test completes or shows 'Interrupted'. Media playback should resume normally after Fit Test ends. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE VALIDATION: Actual (If Bug): Media playback stops permanently, or audio routing gets confused (no sound from either source), or Fit Test result is lost. After navigation round-trip, Fit Test must be in ready state.
